title: acaena minor var. minor
intro: Acaena minor var. minor, also known as the small New Zealand caterpillar plant or woolly-headed caterpillar plant, is a small perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its woolly, silver-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Acaena minor var. minor are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and promoting skin health.
